model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Utah State enters the tournament with a 28-6 record and the momentum of sweeping the Mountain West regular-season and conference tournament titles, led by Mountain West Player of the Year Mason Falslev and Tournament MVP MJ Collins Jr. Villanova boasts a top-40 efficiency profile, but the Aggies' recent form, superior win total, and slight favoritism in the betting markets tip the scales in their favor. In a classic toss-up 8/9 game, Utah State's cohesion and high-level play over the past month provide the edge.

reasoningStep.title: Analyzing Villanova vs. Utah State
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team ratings, recent performance, betting odds, and contextual factors to decide this 8/9 seed matchup.
evidence: Villanova's Torvik rank (36) and KenPom rank (33) indicate a solid top-40 team. Utah State's record (28-6), Mountain West championships, and individual accolades were noted. ESPN Analytics gave Utah State a 51.7% win probability, and the betting line showed Utah State as a slight favorite (-1.5 spread, -125 ML). The absence of Utah State's efficiency rankings in the primary tool was considered, but their strong season and tournament run were deemed compelling.

result.text: Villanova vs. Utah State: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Villanova vs. Utah State: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Villanova vs. Utah State. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Villanova vs. Utah State Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Villanova 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview First-year Villanova head coach Kevin Willard’s team finished 24-8 with a 15-5 Big East record, ranking inside the top 35 in KenPom and the NET. The program is back in the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2022, which is kind of insane for such a high-profile program. Villanova’s defining player is freshman Acaden Lewis, who ended the regular season at 12.3 points, 5.3 assists, 3.1 rebounds, and 1.9 steals per game in 30.5 minutes while carrying the highest usage rate on the team. He is one of the better freshman guards in the country and was the primary reason Villanova climbed nearly 20 spots from its preseason KenPom projection. Tyler Perkins leads the team in scoring at 13.7 points per game while shooting 37% from three, providing the perimeter shooting along with Devin Askew, a 41% marksman. Duke Brennan is 11th nationally in rebounding at just over 10 boards per game, giving the Wildcats a physical interior presence. Depth is an issue after Matt Hodge’s season-ending ACL tear and Zion Stanford’s early departure left Villanova with essentially two rotation players over 6-6. In the Big East Tournament loss to Georgetown, the Hoyas outrebounded them 46-25 in a 14-point defeat. Lewis went 2-for-9. Devin Askew went 1-for-9 from three. When the supporting cast checks out against physical interior opponents or teams with size advantages, the offense stalls and the rebounding becomes a structural problem. Lewis is the reason to believe Villanova can get a first-round win. The depth is the reason to question anything beyond it. Utah State 2026
